source_pr.title: statistics: fix the behavior of DROP STATS after #58596 | tidb-test=pr/2471
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/58181

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

Now that we will not maintain the live updates of the column sizes, the record in stats_histograms will not be re-filled automatically if the `DROP STATS` still drops everything.

To make it correct, change the DROP STATS to light drop.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select table_id from mysql.stats_histograms where stats_ver > 0 group by table_id order by table_id").Check(testkit.Rows(strs...)) (pkg/executor/test/simpletest/simple_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select table_id from mysql.stats_histograms where stats_ver > 0 group by table_id order by table_id").Check(testkit.Rows(strs...)) (pkg/executor/test/simpletest/simple_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The unit test was unstable because it validated stats metadata using results whose row ordering is not guaranteed (and the assertion implicitly depended on a stable order). The fix changes the check to query `mysql.stats_histograms` grouped by `table_id` with an explicit `ORDER BY table_id`, and compares against the actual table/partition IDs, making the assertion deterministic.
fix_pattern: Make the stats metadata assertion deterministic by querying `mysql.stats_histograms` with `ORDER BY table_id` and comparing against computed table/partition IDs (instead of relying on unordered output).

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select table_id from mysql.stats_histograms where stats_ver > 0 group by table_id order by table_id").Check(testkit.Rows(strs...)) (pkg/executor/test/simpletest/simple_test.go)
